In the presence of the Crown Prince of Fujairah, DP World and Fujairah Ports Authority reach an agreement in principle to develop two new terminals on the UAE’s east coast under a 50-year concession. Al Rugaylat Terminal - 2.5 million TEU annually - 1.7 million tonnes of general cargo - 190,000 Car Equivalent Units (CEUs) Dibba Terminal - Adds 3.6 million tonnes of annual general cargo capacity - Development of dedicated logistics zones for both terminals - Construction is expected to take approximately 24 to 30 months from commencement

The Crown Prince of Fujairah witnesses the signing of an agreement between DP World and the Fujairah Ports Authority under a 50-year concession to develop two new terminals: the Al Rugaylat container and multi-purpose terminal and the Dibba General Cargo terminal, in the presence of Essa Kazim, Chairman of DP World, and Capt Mousa Murad, Managing Director of the Fujairah Ports Authority. Al Rugaylat is designed to handle up to 2.5 million TEU annually, alongside 1.7 million tonnes of general cargo and 190,000 Car Equivalent Units (CEUs), while Dibba will add up to 3.6 million tonnes of annual general cargo capacity.
